% Generated by build_identity_cards.py.
% Edit that script if you want these comments or layout changes to survive regeneration.
\documentclass[10pt]{article}

% Landscape letter page arranged as a 2 x 3 sheet of identity cards.
\usepackage[letterpaper,landscape,margin=0.24in]{geometry}
\usepackage[T1]{fontenc}
\usepackage[scaled=0.95]{helvet}
\renewcommand{\familydefault}{\sfdefault}
\usepackage{microtype}
\usepackage{xcolor}
\usepackage{graphicx}
\usepackage[most]{tcolorbox}
\pagestyle{empty}
\setlength{\parindent}{0pt}
\setlength{\parskip}{0pt}
\hyphenpenalty=10000
\exhyphenpenalty=10000
\doublehyphendemerits=100000
\finalhyphendemerits=100000
\emergencystretch=1em

% Shared W.E.T. LAB palette.
\definecolor{agencyblue}{HTML}{005A8B}
\definecolor{deepsea}{HTML}{143B5D}
\definecolor{labline}{HTML}{A9BBC8}
\definecolor{panelbg}{HTML}{F6F8FA}
\definecolor{titlebg}{HTML}{EAF2F7}
\definecolor{textgray}{HTML}{2F3B45}

\color{textgray}
\tcbset{
  sharp corners,
  boxrule=0.6pt,
  colframe=deepsea,
  colback=white,
  left=6pt,
  right=6pt,
  top=4pt,
  bottom=12pt,
  enhanced
}

% Draw one portrait-oriented card in absolute inches.
% Arguments:
%   #1 card ID
%   #2 class label
%   #3 person/species name
%   #4 image path
%   #5 flavor/description text
%   #6 class ability text
\newcommand{\IdentityCardSixPortrait}[6]{%
\begin{tikzpicture}[x=1in,y=1in,baseline=(current bounding box.center)]
% Fixed card shell: outer frame plus header and footer bands.
\fill[white] (0,0) rectangle (2.50,3.21);
\fill[titlebg] (0,3.01) rectangle (2.50,3.21);
\draw[labline,line width=0.35pt] (0,3.01) -- (2.50,3.01);
\fill[panelbg] (0,0) rectangle (2.50,0.15);

% Metadata bands. After rotation, the footer band becomes the narrow side strip.
\node[anchor=west,font=\fontsize{5.0}{5.4}\selectfont\bfseries\color{agencyblue}] at (0.05,3.11) {W.E.T. LAB PERSONNEL DOSSIER};
\node[anchor=west,font=\fontsize{4.0}{4.4}\selectfont\bfseries\color{deepsea}] at (0.06,0.075) {STATION 744 | INTERNAL USE};
\node[anchor=east,font=\fontsize{4.2}{4.6}\selectfont\color{agencyblue}] at (2.44,0.075) {ID-#1};

% Main body stack: class -> image panel -> name -> description -> ability block.
\node[font=\fontsize{21.1}{21.7}\selectfont\bfseries\color{deepsea}] at (1.25,2.79) {#2};

% The card assets are all 900 x 1050, so a 1.248in-tall image is about 1.07in wide.
% This panel adds a nearly even ~0.075in margin around the image on all sides.
\fill[panelbg] (0.64,1.24) rectangle (1.86,2.64);
\draw[labline,line width=0.35pt] (0.64,1.24) rectangle (1.86,2.64);
\node at (1.25,1.94) {\includegraphics[height=1.248in]{#4}};

\node[anchor=north,align=center,text width=2.10in,font=\fontsize{9.25}{9.7}\selectfont\bfseries\color{deepsea}] at (1.25,1.13) {#3};
\node[anchor=north,align=center,text width=2.10in,font=\fontsize{6.25}{6.55}\selectfont\color{textgray}] at (1.25,0.84) {#5};
\draw[labline,line width=0.3pt] (0.24,0.48) -- (2.26,0.48);
\node[anchor=north,align=center,text width=2.06in,font=\fontsize{5.92}{6.2}\selectfont\itshape\color{deepsea}] at (1.25,0.45) {#6};

\draw[deepsea, line width=0.75pt] (0,0) rectangle (2.50,3.21);
\end{tikzpicture}%
}

% Raster cell wrapper. We draw the card upright above, then rotate it here
% so the 6-up sheet keeps the same portrait-card proportions as the 4-up version.
\newcommand{\IdentityCardSix}[6]{%
\begin{tcolorbox}[
  blankest,
  width=\linewidth,
  height=2.58in,
  colback=white,
  colframe=white,
  boxrule=0pt,
  boxsep=0pt,
  left=0pt,
  right=0pt,
  top=0pt,
  bottom=0pt
]
\vspace*{\fill}
\makebox[\linewidth][c]{%
\rotatebox[origin=c]{-90}{%
\IdentityCardSixPortrait{#1}{#2}{#3}{#4}{#5}{#6}%
}%
}
\vspace*{\fill}
\end{tcolorbox}%
}

\begin{document}
\small

% One landscape page = 2 columns x 3 rows = 6 cards.
\begin{tcbraster}[raster columns=2,raster column skip=8pt,raster row skip=6pt]
\IdentityCardSix{01}{Geneticist}{Dr. Mason Wren\\[-0.01in]{\fontsize{6.6}{6.9}\selectfont (Alien Resurrection)}}{card_assets/card_01.png}{The lead scientist attempting to clone Ripley and domesticate the galaxy's most lethal parasite.}{May engineer one hybrid organism; the hybrid counts as both species during play.}
\IdentityCardSix{02}{Geneticist}{Barbara McClintock\\[-0.01in]{\fontsize{6.6}{6.9}\selectfont (1902 - 1992)}}{card_assets/card_02.png}{A Nobel Prize-winning geneticist who discovered "jumping genes" (transposable elements).}{May engineer one hybrid organism; the hybrid counts as both species during play.}
\IdentityCardSix{03}{Geneticist}{Rosalind Franklin\\[-0.01in]{\fontsize{6.6}{6.9}\selectfont (1920 - 1958)}}{card_assets/card_03.png}{A chemist whose X-ray diffraction image of DNA was so good it basically solved the double helix puzzle by itself.}{May engineer one hybrid organism; the hybrid counts as both species during play.}
\IdentityCardSix{04}{Geneticist}{Dr. Henry Wu\\[-0.01in]{\fontsize{6.6}{6.9}\selectfont (Jurassic World)}}{card_assets/card_04.png}{A geneticist who cloned dinosaurs and then decided they were not exciting enough, so he started inventing new ones.}{May engineer one hybrid organism; the hybrid counts as both species during play.}
\IdentityCardSix{05}{Geneticist}{Tyler Hutchison\\[-0.01in]{\fontsize{6.6}{6.9}\selectfont (Present)}}{card_assets/card_05.png}{A Davis-based geneticist who develops advanced molecular diagnostics while somehow maintaining the energy to scale rock walls and survive desert festivals.}{May engineer one hybrid organism; the hybrid counts as both species during play.}
\IdentityCardSix{06}{Geneticist}{Gregor Mendel\\[-0.01in]{\fontsize{6.6}{6.9}\selectfont (1822 - 1884)}}{card_assets/card_06.png}{An Augustinian monk who spent years breeding pea plants and accidentally invented modern genetics.}{May engineer one hybrid organism; the hybrid counts as both species during play.}
\end{tcbraster}

\IdentityCardSix{07}{Geneticist}{Eldon Tyrell\\[-0.01in]{\fontsize{6.6}{6.9}\selectfont (Blade Runner)}}{card_assets/card_07.png}{The CEO-scientist who manufactures artificial humans and calls them a premium product line. Tyrell proudly describes replicants as, "More human than human."}{May engineer one hybrid organism; the hybrid counts as both species during play.}
\IdentityCardSix{08}{Data Scientist}{Alan Turing\\[-0.01in]{\fontsize{6.6}{6.9}\selectfont (1912 - 1954)}}{card_assets/card_08.png}{A mathematician and codebreaker whose work laid the foundations of modern computing.}{May declare "model overfit" before a mini-game; a successful result doubles that station payout.}
\IdentityCardSix{09}{Data Scientist}{Derek Zoolander and Hansel\\[-0.01in]{\fontsize{6.6}{6.9}\selectfont (Zoolander)}}{card_assets/card_09.png}{Two supermodels with extraordinary cheekbones and limited analytical range who still approach every problem with complete conviction.}{May declare "model overfit" before a mini-game; a successful result doubles that station payout.}
\IdentityCardSix{10}{Data Scientist}{W.E.B. Du Bois\\[-0.01in]{\fontsize{6.6}{6.9}\selectfont (1868-1963)}}{card_assets/card_10.png}{A groundbreaking sociologist and the first African American to earn a Ph.D. from Harvard University.}{May declare "model overfit" before a mini-game; a successful result doubles that station payout.}
\IdentityCardSix{11}{Data Scientist}{Ada Lovelace\\[-0.01in]{\fontsize{6.6}{6.9}\selectfont (1815 - 1852)}}{card_assets/card_11.png}{A mathematician who realized Babbage's machine could do more than arithmetic and drafted one of the earliest computer algorithms.}{May declare "model overfit" before a mini-game; a successful result doubles that station payout.}
\IdentityCardSix{12}{Data Scientist}{Florence Nightingale\\[-0.01in]{\fontsize{6.6}{6.9}\selectfont (1820 - 1910)}}{card_assets/card_12.png}{A nurse and statistician who transformed hospital sanitation and used data graphics to expose preventable military deaths.}{May declare "model overfit" before a mini-game; a successful result doubles that station payout.}

\IdentityCardSix{13}{Data Scientist}{Dr. Matt Savoca\\[-0.01in]{\fontsize{6.6}{6.9}\selectfont (Present)}}{card_assets/card_13.png}{A marine ecologist whose enthusiasm for whales is so powerful he can talk about them indefinitely to anyone within conversational range.}{May declare "model overfit" before a mini-game; a successful result doubles that station payout.}
\IdentityCardSix{14}{Data Scientist}{Dr. Gladys West\\[-0.01in]{\fontsize{6.6}{6.9}\selectfont (1930 - 2026)}}{card_assets/card_14.png}{A pioneering mathematician whose geodesy work for the U.S. Navy helped establish the mathematical foundation for GPS.}{May declare "model overfit" before a mini-game; a successful result doubles that station payout.}
\IdentityCardSix{15}{Oceanographer}{Jacques Cousteau\\[-0.01in]{\fontsize{6.6}{6.9}\selectfont (1910 - 1997)}}{card_assets/card_15.png}{The legendary explorer who turned underwater exploration into a global documentary adventure.}{Receives three clues to hidden \$100 discoveries and may search for them during the game phase.}
\IdentityCardSix{16}{Oceanographer}{Matt Hooper\\[-0.01in]{\fontsize{6.6}{6.9}\selectfont (Jaws)}}{card_assets/card_16.png}{A polite marine biologist who shows up to study a shark and ends up hunting it with a fisherman and a police chief.}{Receives three clues to hidden \$100 discoveries and may search for them during the game phase.}
\IdentityCardSix{17}{Oceanographer}{Rachel Carson\\[-0.01in]{\fontsize{6.6}{6.9}\selectfont (1907 - 1964)}}{card_assets/card_17.png}{A marine biologist and writer who politely informed the world that dumping chemicals everywhere might be a catastrophically bad ecological strategy.}{Receives three clues to hidden \$100 discoveries and may search for them during the game phase.}
\IdentityCardSix{18}{Oceanographer}{Sylvia Earle\\[-0.01in]{\fontsize{6.6}{6.9}\selectfont (1935 - Present)}}{card_assets/card_18.png}{A marine biologist who has spent more time underwater than most people spend thinking about the ocean.}{Receives three clues to hidden \$100 discoveries and may search for them during the game phase.}
\end{tcbraster}

\IdentityCardSix{20}{Oceanographer}{Steve Zissou\\[-0.01in]{\fontsize{6.6}{6.9}\selectfont (The Life Aquatic with Steve Zissou)}}{card_assets/card_20.png}{A washed-up oceanographer on a mission to locate and avenge a possibly fictional Jaguar Shark.}{Receives three clues to hidden \$100 discoveries and may search for them during the game phase.}
\IdentityCardSix{21}{Oceanographer}{Dr. Susan McCallister\\[-0.01in]{\fontsize{6.6}{6.9}\selectfont (Deep Blue Sea)}}{card_assets/card_21.png}{A scientist attempting to cure Alzheimer's disease by making sharks dramatically smarter.}{Receives three clues to hidden \$100 discoveries and may search for them during the game phase.}
\IdentityCardSix{22}{P.I.}{Ian Malcolm\\[-0.01in]{\fontsize{6.6}{6.9}\selectfont (Jurassic Park)}}{card_assets/card_22.png}{A chaos theorist who attends dangerous science experiments solely to say "I told you so".}{Maintains the team's notebook and records wins, discoveries, and collaborations; valid entries require an opposing team signature.}
\IdentityCardSix{23}{P.I.}{Guy Taylor\\[-0.01in]{\fontsize{6.6}{6.9}\selectfont (Good Luck, Have Fun, Don't Die)}}{card_assets/card_23.png}{A time-traveling scientist who returns from the future to prevent humanity from destroying itself.}{Maintains the team's notebook and records wins, discoveries, and collaborations; valid entries require an opposing team signature.}
\IdentityCardSix{24}{P.I.}{Dr. Frederick Frankenstein\\[-0.01in]{\fontsize{6.6}{6.9}\selectfont (Young Frankenstein)}}{card_assets/card_24.png}{A scientist who insists his name is pronounced "Fronk-en-steen" to avoid association with his family's corpse-reanimation business.}{Maintains the team's notebook and records wins, discoveries, and collaborations; valid entries require an opposing team signature.}

\IdentityCardSix{25}{Whale}{Beluga Whale\\[-0.01in]{\fontsize{6.6}{6.9}\selectfont (\textit{Delphinapterus leucas})}}{card_assets/card_25.png}{A smiling Arctic whale that looks friendly but also appears to know something about you.}{Grants the team a \$200 prestige advantage for each whale assigned to the roster.}
\IdentityCardSix{26}{Whale}{Common Minke whale\\[-0.01in]{\fontsize{6.6}{6.9}\selectfont (\textit{Balaenoptera acutorostrata})}}{card_assets/card_26.png}{The smallest species of rorqual whale and the second-smallest baleen whale in the world.}{Grants the team a \$200 prestige advantage for each whale assigned to the roster.}
\IdentityCardSix{27}{Whale}{Orca Whale\\[-0.01in]{\fontsize{6.6}{6.9}\selectfont (\textit{Orcinus Orca})}}{card_assets/card_27.png}{A highly intelligent apex predator that hunts in coordinated pods and occasionally treats sharks like a convenient snack option.}{Grants the team a \$200 prestige advantage for each whale assigned to the roster.}
\IdentityCardSix{28}{Whale}{Blue Whale\\[-0.01in]{\fontsize{6.6}{6.9}\selectfont (\textit{Balaenoptera musculus})}}{card_assets/card_28.png}{The largest animal to ever exist on Earth- a creature so enormous that an entire Tyrannosaurus rex could probably wait politely in line behind its tongue.}{Grants the team a \$200 prestige advantage for each whale assigned to the roster.}
\IdentityCardSix{29}{Whale}{Narwhal\\[-0.01in]{\fontsize{6.6}{6.9}\selectfont (\textit{Mondon monoceros})}}{card_assets/card_29.png}{An Arctic-dwelling toothed whale best known for the long, spiraling tusk that projects from the heads of males, earning it the nickname "unicorn of the sea".}{Grants the team a \$200 prestige advantage for each whale assigned to the roster.}
\IdentityCardSix{30}{Whale}{Humpback whales\\[-0.01in]{\fontsize{6.6}{6.9}\selectfont (\textit{Megaptera novaeangliae})}}{card_assets/card_30.png}{Large baleen whales famous for their long-distance migrations, complex songs, and acrobatic surface behaviors like breaching.}{Grants the team a \$200 prestige advantage for each whale assigned to the roster.}
\end{tcbraster}
\newpage
% One landscape page = 2 columns x 3 rows = 6 cards.
\begin{tcbraster}[raster columns=2,raster column skip=8pt,raster row skip=6pt]
\IdentityCardSix{31}{Whale}{Sperm whale\\[-0.01in]{\fontsize{6.6}{6.9}\selectfont (\textit{Physeter macrocephalus})}}{card_assets/card_31.png}{The largest of the toothed whales and the largest toothed predator on Earth.}{Grants the team a \$200 prestige advantage for each whale assigned to the roster.}
\IdentityCardSix{32}{Dolphin}{Risso's dolphin\\[-0.01in]{\fontsize{6.6}{6.9}\selectfont (\textit{Grampus griseus})}}{card_assets/card_32.png}{A large, unique species of dolphin famous for its heavily scarred appearance and blunt, bulbous head.}{Acts as the team's dolphin during Dolphin Trainer and may respond only to approved cue phrases.}
\IdentityCardSix{33}{Dolphin}{Atlantic Spotted dolphin\\[-0.01in]{\fontsize{6.6}{6.9}\selectfont (\textit{Stenella frontalis})}}{card_assets/card_33.png}{A warm-water Atlantic dolphin whose spots accumulate with age, shifting from smooth gray juveniles to heavily speckled adults.}{Acts as the team's dolphin during Dolphin Trainer and may respond only to approved cue phrases.}
\IdentityCardSix{34}{Dolphin}{Dusky Dolphin\\[-0.01in]{\fontsize{6.6}{6.9}\selectfont (\textit{Lagenorhynchus obscurus})}}{card_assets/card_34.png}{A small, highly social species famous for its incredible acrobatics.}{Acts as the team's dolphin during Dolphin Trainer and may respond only to approved cue phrases.}
\IdentityCardSix{35}{Dolphin}{Pacific white-sided dolphin\\[-0.01in]{\fontsize{6.6}{6.9}\selectfont (\textit{Aethalodelphis obliquidens})}}{card_assets/card_35.png}{Often called a "lag" after its former genus Lagenorhynchus, is an energetic and highly social marine mammal found in the temperate waters of the North Pacific.}{Acts as the team's dolphin during Dolphin Trainer and may respond only to approved cue phrases.}
\IdentityCardSix{36}{Dolphin}{Bottlenose Dolphin\\[-0.01in]{\fontsize{6.6}{6.9}\selectfont (\textit{Tursiops truncatus})}}{card_assets/card_36.png}{The most famous dolphin in television history, widely believed to have personally solved more crimes than most police departments.}{Acts as the team's dolphin during Dolphin Trainer and may respond only to approved cue phrases.}
\end{tcbraster}

\IdentityCardSix{37}{Dolphin}{Amazon River Dolphin\\[-0.01in]{\fontsize{6.6}{6.9}\selectfont (\textit{Inia geoffrensis})}}{card_assets/card_37.png}{A pink freshwater dolphin that looks like a normal dolphin until you realize it lives in a jungle river and has a flexible neck like a snake.}{Acts as the team's dolphin during Dolphin Trainer and may respond only to approved cue phrases.}
\IdentityCardSix{38}{Dolphin}{Irrawaddy Dolphin\\[-0.01in]{\fontsize{6.6}{6.9}\selectfont (\textit{Orcaella brevirostris})}}{card_assets/card_38.png}{A rare, charismatic species of oceanic dolphin known for its distinctive rounded head, flexible neck, and "smiling" expression.}{Acts as the team's dolphin during Dolphin Trainer and may respond only to approved cue phrases.}
\IdentityCardSix{39}{Squid}{Joubin's Squid\\[-0.01in]{\fontsize{6.6}{6.9}\selectfont (\textit{Joubiniteuthis portieri})}}{card_assets/card_39.png}{An exceptionally rare deep-sea cephalopod and the only member of the family Joubiniteuthidae.}{At the end of the game, may siphon \$200 from any rival team.}
\IdentityCardSix{40}{Squid}{Vampire Squid\\[-0.01in]{\fontsize{6.6}{6.9}\selectfont (\textit{Vampyroteuthis infernalis})}}{card_assets/card_40.png}{A unique, small deep-sea cephalopod found at 600-1,200m in tropical/temperate oceans.}{At the end of the game, may siphon \$200 from any rival team.}
\IdentityCardSix{41}{Squid}{Firefly Squid\\[-0.01in]{\fontsize{6.6}{6.9}\selectfont (\textit{Watasenia scintillans})}}{card_assets/card_41.png}{A tiny deep-sea cephalopod famous for its brilliant blue bioluminescence.}{At the end of the game, may siphon \$200 from any rival team.}
\IdentityCardSix{42}{Squid}{Giant Squid\\[-0.01in]{\fontsize{6.6}{6.9}\selectfont (\textit{\textit{Architeuthis dux}})}}{card_assets/card_42.png}{A deep-sea cryptid that occasionally surfaces to remind humanity it is not the apex predator.}{At the end of the game, may siphon \$200 from any rival team.}

\IdentityCardSix{44}{Squid}{Southern Pygmy Squid\\[-0.01in]{\fontsize{6.6}{6.9}\selectfont (\textit{Xipholeptos notoides})}}{card_assets/card_44.png}{The smallest of all cephalopods (i.e., squid, octopus, cuttlefish, nautilus) growing only to about 1.6 cm (less than 3/4 inch) in length.}{At the end of the game, may siphon \$200 from any rival team.}
\IdentityCardSix{45}{Squid}{Tropical Bobbletail Squid\\[-0.01in]{\fontsize{6.6}{6.9}\selectfont (\textit{Sepiadarium kochi})}}{card_assets/card_45.png}{A small Indo-West Pacific bobtail squid that buries itself in sand by day, shifts from orange to pale white, and releases heavy defensive slime.}{At the end of the game, may siphon \$200 from any rival team.}
\IdentityCardSix{46}{Admin}{Dr. Scott Coffin\\[-0.01in]{\fontsize{6.6}{6.9}\selectfont (Present)}}{card_assets/card_46.png}{The W.E.T. LAB's best (and only) environmental toxicologist.}{May alter rules, reassign specimens, redistribute funding, and declare any outcome scientifically necessary.}
